title: Cabling Families of Legendrian Embeddings
speaker: Eduardo Fernández
abstract: I will discuss a recursive formula for the homotopy type of the space of Legendrian embeddings of sufficiently positive cables with the maximal Thurston-Bennequin invariant. Via this formula, we identify infinitely many new components within the space of Legendrian embeddings in the standard contact 3-sphere that satisfy an injective h-principle. These components include those containing positive Legendrian torus knots with the maximal Thurston-Bennequin invariant. This work is a collaboration with Hyunki Min.

proof_stages.label: Legendrian versus formal embedding problem
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines Legendrian and formal Legendrian embedding spaces in standard contact S^3, reviews what is known about the inclusion on homotopy groups, and motivates the search for formally trivial but nontrivial Legendrian families.

proof_stages.label: Sufficiently positive cabling and the first equivalence
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines (p,q) and n(p,q) cables, sufficient positivity, the generalized K�e1lm�e1n loop, and states the knot-level theorem that a sufficiently positive maximal-tb cable has Legendrian embedding space weakly equivalent to S^1 times the space for the underlying Legendrian.

proof_stages.label: Generalized link cables and the C-property theorem
proof_stages.summary: Cabling is generalized to links, the C-property is defined via contractibility of the contact-structure space on the complement, and the main theorem states that sufficiently positive maximal generalized cables preserve this complement homotopy type and hence preserve injectivity of the formal-inclusion map.

proof_stages.label: Reduction from Legendrian spaces to contact structures on complements
proof_stages.summary: Using long embeddings, evaluation fibrations, contactomorphism orbit fibrations, the EliashbergMishachev contractibility theorem, and Gray stability, the study of Legendrian embedding spaces is reduced to studying spaces of contact structures on Legendrian complements.

proof_stages.label: Convex-surface and bypass control for cable complements
proof_stages.summary: Convex surface theory, dividing sets, flexibility, and bypasses are reviewed and then applied to the cable torus to obtain standard annuli cutting the cable complement into standard neighborhoods and the complement of the underlying Legendrian.

proof_stages.label: Standard-annulus weak equivalence and gluing theorem
proof_stages.summary: A technical theorem identifies the space of n-standard annuli with the corresponding smooth annulus embedding space under twisting/no-bypass hypotheses, and its corollary says that gluing along such annuli does not change the homotopy type of the tight contact-structure space.

proof_stages.label: Application of gluing to prove cable preservation
proof_stages.summary: The annulus decomposition and gluing theorem are applied to the complement of a sufficiently positive cable to prove that its space of contact structures is weakly equivalent to that of the underlying Legendrian complement.

proof_stages.label: Microfibration proof of the annulus theorem
proof_stages.summary: The technical annulus weak equivalence is proved by a microfibration criterion: density follows from the absence of nontrivial bypasses, while local equivalence follows from known contactomorphism groups of standard tight solid tori.

examples.name: Iterated sufficiently positive cables of the maximal-tb Legendrian unknot
examples.note: Starting from the tb = -1 Legendrian unknot, repeated sufficiently positive cabling gives an explicit homotopy-type computation for the resulting Legendrian embedding spaces, illustrating the first main theorem.
examples.engagement_reason: he applies the main theorem to iterated sufficiently positive cables of the tb=-1 Legendrian unknot to obtain a concrete homotopy-type description of their Legendrian embedding spaces
